newsArray.title: ‘Sir Garfield was more than the greatest’ - Dehring says CWI mourns, celebrates Sobers along with Barbados
newsArray.description: Cricket West Indies joins the people of Barbados, the wider Caribbean, and the global cricket community in mourning the passing of The Right Excellent Sir Garfield Sobers, a towering figure whose influence on our game and our region can never be overstated.

newsArray.title: Adieu Sir Garfield Sobers, You Will Always Live In The Hearts Of Cricket Fans As A Legend
newsArray.description: Legendary West Indies all-rounder Sir Garfield Sobers died in Barbados at 89, drawing tributes from cricket figures including Sunil Gavaskar. Known as a five-in-one cricketer, Sobers excelled in batting, bowling, fielding and captaincy. He scored 8,032 Test runs and held the record for a 365 not out innings, leaving a lasting legacy in cricket.
creator: editorial

newsArray.title: 39-Year-Old Rohit Sharma Turns Back The Clock With Stunning Dive At Lord's
newsArray.description: Rohit Sharma showcased his enduring talent in the series-deciding ODI against England at Lord's, silencing doubts about his future in cricket. The 39-year-old executed a stunning dive to prevent a boundary, igniting cheers from the crowd.
creator: nikhil

newsArray.title: Joe Root scripts world record, becomes first England batter to register six consecutive 50-plus ODI scores
newsArray.description: Joe Root continued his dream run against India, smashing another unbeaten fifty to script multiple records at Lord's. The England star became the first batter from his country to register six consecutive 50-plus scores in ODI cricket while also setting a new world record.
creator: riya mishra published jul ; updated jul
